# Transcrição: 08- Delivery Insights

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hi, so today I want to talk to you guys about delivery insights. Now if you don't know how to access this or you've never seen this, I'm going to take you back into Ads Manager to show you how to access this dashboard which shows you how you're performing in the auction process and how to improve what you're doing. So delivery insights takes place at the ad set level and you're going to need at least a impressions of 500 and it doesn't always show all the time but what happens is when you scroll under the delivery column here, if there's enough data available it will give you the opportunity to see your delivery insights. So we're going to go ahead and click on that. Now once you hit the dashboard you're going to see sort of a comparison graph over a time frame and it's going to show you your reach as well as the amount of money that you are spending. So you can get a quick overview of how many people you're reaching, is it costing you less, is it costing you more. I'm going to take you through these tabs and quickly explain what they are referring to. So obviously here these are the days, the date that you're looking at here is the previous day so this would not be today just FYI, total impressions, first time impression ratio. So this is unique people seeing your ad for the first time. Now if this starts to go down consistently it means that you're starting to get fatigue and it's time to expand your audience and go a little bit wider. In this situation here you can see it's actually fluctuating up and down so there's not much that we need to be concerned of at the moment for this but if this started to consistently go down over say three, four, five days then we would start to expand our audiences. This is your reach here and then this is the reached ratio. Now I've got nothing in here because I don't have enough data but this will basically tell you how many people within your audience that you have actually reached the percentage of that. Once we go into activity history over here you'll see we've also got our dates and these are basically changes. You can see this in your ads manager under the edit history. This is pretty much the same thing. Auction overlap is a great tab to show you how you're doing inside of the auction. So over here we've got our spend, our dates and then here we've got the total number, the percentage overlap that our ad sets are competing against each other. Now what this means is let's say I've got an overlap of 14%. This means that this specific ad set went into 14% fewer auctions because it was competing with this one over here and then it shows you you know the first the the most overlapping ad set, the second most overlapping. When you see something that's a really high overlap like this and I would say anything over about sort of 5% to 10% is a

## [03:00 - 05:13]

significant overlap because it's going to affect the reach and how many auctions you get shown into. You're almost better off combining those targeting options together under the same ad set. So putting whatever targeting you have together you want to see something like this, no overlap or just a very very minimal overlap like that which will ensure that your ad sets get shown to the most people. Finally auction competition is all about your bid and how competitive your essentially your targeting is. So again we've got our date, this is the lowest cap bid that you choose when you select your lowest bid in the auction in your ad set level and then this is essentially how much it's costing for that bid. Now over here we've got auction competition and if you're seeing that you have for example 15% more competitive so what this is referring to is on a day-to-day basis how much more competitive or less competitive is your targeting. What are the fluctuations that are happening there? And so if you see something like 15% more competitive it means that your bid is going to go up slightly during this time and you can see this happening over here in the paced auction bid the actual bid that you placed on this specific day. Now if it's over 20% this is a significant amount and this is time to start reconsidering how much you put as your actual bid. So what you look at is how much am I actually paying for winning this auction? How competitive is this auction? Am I even stacking up? And based on that you can pretty much edit your own bid that you set within the ad set level. Over here finally I'm just going to show you this tab up at the top so we've got our amount spent, we've got our cost per result here and then we've got results and impressions over here but this is really the main stuff that you'd be looking and you can obviously export your data. So this is how to look at the delivery insights tool and how to interpret the information that you're seeing.
